summary:
In performing a radical neck dissection, most specified anatomical elements are typically addressed except possibly part of the parotid gland due to its critical role nearby; thus, ensuring minimal disruption while effectively managing disease risk around this region. The exact boundaries can vary based on surgical approach but generally leave behind non-affected parts necessary for function preservation near vital tissues. Agent1 reasoned through each structural element’s usual fate post-surgery, concluding with an emphasis on preserving essential functions despite extensive tissue manipulation required clinically when dealing with potential cancers spreading into these regions. 

The summary focuses only on key aspects related to identifying preserved vs surgically altered components within radical neck dissection contexts without extraneous details about specific nerves or glands beyond their relevance here. It highlights how partial retention aligns more closely than full extirpation under normal protocols aimed at both therapeutic efficacy alongside patient care considerations regarding facial integrity maintenance whenever feasible medically speaking.
